title: Tabernaemontana psorocarpa
taxonomy: Tabernaemontana psorocarpa is classified in the family Apocynaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family is a member of the order Gentianales and the clade Asterids within the angiosperms. The genus Tabernaemontana is a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and the Pacific. The species psorocarpa is one of the many species within the genus, known for its unique characteristics and distribution. The classification of Tabernaemontana psorocarpa reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Apocynaceae family.

intro: Tabernaemontana psorocarpa, also known as the yellow staminate Tabernaemontana, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic activities.
